rebaseline_note: Treated the pre-existing Codex partial tree as untrusted. Verified each partial module/test against the approved plan interfaces + assertions, ran required_tests, executed the hooks for fail-open, and ran adversarial negative-case checks (Codex-no-Skill(), owner-boundary, builder-job path guard) before trusting. Committed ONLY allowed_paths files. One out-of-scope diff (FORK-MAINTENANCE.md) left untouched and reported below.

tests.summary: ALL CHECKS PASSED: 51 unit tests; inline smoke (FSM compact_progress, visual-artifact policy, skill-invocation adapter, builder-job validator, test-design.json lint 60 cases, test-design.md parity); no active writing-arch refs; S2 verify artifacts clean.
196 passed — full runtime suite (52 new + 144 existing); no regression.
vmodel contract tests passed (skill edits did not break contract greps).

blocked.summary: The plan's SFSM-05 writing-arch drift cleanup (Task 5 step 4) requires editing FORK-MAINTENANCE.md, but FORK-MAINTENANCE.md is NOT in allowed_paths. The pre-existing Codex partial tree already modified it (2 lines: removed 'native writing-arch routing' from the brainstorming bullet + marked skills/writing-arch/ 'superseded/historical'). Per the handoff rule ('report out-of-scope diff instead of modifying it'), I did NOT commit or revert it — it remains modified in the working tree, uncommitted.
blocked.coupling: The committed harness tests/test-superpower-fsm-runtime.ps1 (allowed_path) scans FORK-MAINTENANCE.md for active writing-arch refs (line ~114). It passes NOW because the working tree carries the Codex FORK-MAINTENANCE.md edit. If FORK-MAINTENANCE.md is reverted to HEAD (unmarked 'native writing-arch routing' + 'skills/writing-arch/ (new)'), required_test 2 will FAIL on a clean checkout.
blocked.recommended_resolution: Orchestrator: either (a) add FORK-MAINTENANCE.md to allowed_paths so the SFSM-05 cleanup commits alongside the harness that enforces it (preferred — keeps the deliverable self-consistent), or (b) apply the 2-line FORK-MAINTENANCE.md marking yourself, or (c) narrow the harness's writing-arch scan to drop FORK-MAINTENANCE.md (weakens the plan's lint — not recommended). No other unmarked writing-arch reference exists in the repo (registry.yaml absent; skills/hooks/lib/tests/docs clean).
